chips.text: work of breathing documented if assessed
MRC dyspnea grade documented if assessed
cough, wheeze and chest pain reviewed

speed_preset.text: work of breathing documented if assessed

history_draft.text: Patient presents with shortness of breath for [onset/duration]. [Exertional vs rest]. [Orthopnea/PND]. [Cough/wheeze/chest pain]. [Leg swelling]. [Additional history].

exam_prompts.text: Documented if assessed or measured. Oxygen saturation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Respiratory rate documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Blood pressure documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Chest auscultation documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Wheeze or crackles documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. Peripheral oedema documented.
Documented if assessed or measured. JVP documented.

investigation_options.text: Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered ECG reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Oxygen saturation reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Chest X-ray reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered Blood tests reviewed.
Reviewed if relevant. Reviewed. Clinician-entered BNP/NT-proBNP reviewed.

plan_options.text: Clinician-entered plan documented documented if clinician decided.

clinical_blockers: Children, acute or subacute dyspnoea, and emergency treatment are outside the exact pathway scope.
Syncope, fever, unilateral chest signs, tracheal position, previous PFT, generic clinician plans, generic follow-up, fixed follow-up, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, investigation interpretation, urgency, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
